title: Antibiotic Selection
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"ort2","text":"Confirm that E. coli DH10B has successfully taken up the plasmid carrying the synthetic EcOrep operon, and select colonies with the antibiotic resistance marker via antibiotic selection.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":13,"length":7}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"6fkmu","text":"Recovered culture (per tube: 100 µL competent cells + 6 µL plasmid + 1 mL SOB medium; recover at 37 °C for 2 h)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7qmh","text":"LB agar powder (containing 1% tryptone, 0.5% yeast extract, 1% NaCl, 2% agar)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1javi","text":"[Antibiotic] stock solution: 20 mg/mL, dissolved in 100% ethanol, stored at −20 °C","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"arqel","text":"ddH₂O","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bun4p","text":"LB agar plates: final [Antibiotic] concentration 20 µg/mL per plate (see preparation steps below)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bgt88","text":"Sterile 1.5 mL Eppendorf tubes","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"nkr7","text":"100 mm sterile Petri dishes","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9dvob","text":"Sterile glass spreader","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5cjp6","text":"Micropipettes (20 µL, 200 µL, 1000 µL)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"53gtr","text":"37 °C incubator","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Weigh 25 g LB agar powder using an electronic balance and dissolve in 1 L ddH₂O.
After autoclaving, cool the medium to ~50 °C (check with an infrared thermometer).
Add 1 mL of 20 mg/mL [Antibiotic] stock to 1 L of medium (final concentration: 20 µg/mL) and mix thoroughly. ( Ensure the medium has cooled sufficiently before adding antibiotic. )
Pour 25 mL of the medium into each sterile Petri dish.
Allow to solidify at room temperature. Seal with Parafilm, label with date and antibiotic name, and store at 4 °C (up to 7 days).
Mix the recovered culture thoroughly, then split into two groups: Undiluted group: Take 100 µL of undiluted culture. 1:10 dilution group: Mix 100 µL culture with 900 µL SOB; from this, take 100 µL.
Spread each 100 µL sample evenly onto LB agar plates containing the antibiotic.
Use a sterile glass spreader to distribute the liquid.
Let the surface dry (~5 minutes), then invert the plates.
Place the plates in a 37 °C incubator, inverted, and incubate for 14–16 hours.

On the following day, observe colony formation: Presence of colonies indicates successful transformation. If colonies are too dense on the undiluted plate, use colonies from the 1:10 dilution plate instead (adjust depending on culture density).
creator: igemnthuth
